description_long: This is a pair of traditional Japanese geta sandals, featuring thick, rectangular wooden soles painted in a vibrant red color. The sandals have two prominent black fabric thongs (hanao) that loop through the wooden base, allowing the foot to be secured. Each sandal is elevated by two wooden teeth (ha) on the underside, giving them a distinctive raised profile. The wood shows some signs of wear and minor scuffing, suggesting regular use. Brass or metallic fasteners are visible where the straps attach to the wood, adding a functional accent. The overall appearance is utilitarian yet striking, with a bold color contrast and a sturdy, handcrafted look.
description: A pair of red wooden Japanese geta sandals with black fabric thongs and raised wooden teeth, showing signs of use and sturdy construction.
description_view_0: Front view showing the black fabric thongs and the tops of the red wooden soles, with the sandals slightly angled toward each other.
description_view_1: Angled front-left view highlighting the thickness of the wooden soles and the attachment points of the black straps.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the two raised wooden teeth on each sandal and the solid construction of the base.
description_view_3: Angled rear-right view emphasizing the back of the sandals and the visible wear on the wooden surfaces.
